mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month trend in PAYEMS signals that firms are cutting labor input before earnings expectations fully adjust, so equity risk premia rise and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative.
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 137993 thousand persons as of February 2008
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid fall in FEDFUNDS during a slowing economy signals emergency policy easing driven by recession and credit stress rather than benign discount-rate relief, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses more than 1.0 percentage point below its 6-month average
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 2.98 percent as of February 2008
regime_hint: emergency monetary easing during growth slowdown
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS declines are followed by average 20-day S&P 500 returns that are positive and not below unconditional returns, or if the negative hit rate is below 50% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in HOUST and RSAFS indicates that the housing contraction is spilling into household spending, reducing expected revenues and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
series:RSAFS
fact:HOUST latest available value was 1012 thousand units as of January 2008
fact:RSAFS latest available value was 380197 million dollars as of February 2008
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST and RSAFS trigger does not produce lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than either single-series signal alone, or if triggered returns are positive in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A downside crossing in INDPRO signals that production, inventories, and operating leverage are deteriorating, causing near-term earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest available value was 114.2334 as of January 2008
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside crossings have no negative mean 5-day S&P 500 return relative to unconditional returns, or if the one-sided t-test for underperformance has p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL alongside falling FEDFUNDS indicates inflation pressure during policy easing, a stagflationary mix that limits real policy relief and compresses equity multiples, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth > 3% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change < -0.5 percentage point
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 212.571 as of February 2008
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 2.98 percent as of February 2008
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction has no incremental explanatory power over FEDFUNDS alone, or if triggered 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 momentum indicates that aggregate real demand is still expanding, supporting corporate cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when the expansion signal remains intact.
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 11677.1 billions of chained 2017 dollars as of 2007Q4
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 4-quarter growth is not associated with positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal underperforms unconditional returns by more than 100 basis points annualized.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and rising recession probability, leading investors to cut earnings forecasts and requiring negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage point above its 6-month minimum
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.8 percent as of February 2008
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE upward crossings are followed by nonnegative mean 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the negative-return hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below prior-cycle norms signals collapsing residential investment and bank collateral stress, which lowers credit-sensitive earnings expectations and produces negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest available value was 1012 thousand units as of January 2008
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings below 1100 thousand units are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.
